title: Tech and Auto Industries Face Major Changes
subtitle: Companies are changing how they work as artificial intelligence grows and global competition increases.

summary.en: Tech companies are removing middle management layers to become more efficient with artificial intelligence. This shift is changing the daily responsibilities of many employees. Meanwhile, European carmakers are struggling with lower sales and are now working with Chinese competitors. These manufacturers are selling or sharing their factory space to stay in business. Both the tech and auto sectors are facing difficult decisions to remain competitive in a changing global economy.
summary.zh: 科技公司正透過移除中階管理層級，利用人工智慧提升效率。這種轉變正在改變許多員工的日常職責。與此同時，歐洲車廠正因銷量下滑而陷入困境，目前正與中國競爭對手合作。這些製造商透過出售或分享廠房空間來維持營運。科技與汽車產業皆面臨艱難的決策，以在不斷變化的全球經濟中保持競爭力。

paragraphs.en: The global economy is currently going through a period of fast and difficult change. In the technology sector, companies are using artificial intelligence (AI) to rethink how they operate. At the same time, traditional car manufacturers in Europe are facing new pressure from strong competitors in China. These two trends are forcing businesses to make big changes to their structures and strategies.
paragraphs.zh: 全球經濟目前正經歷一段快速且艱難的變革期。在科技產業，企業正利用人工智慧重新思考其營運模式。與此同時，歐洲的傳統汽車製造商正面臨來自中國強大競爭對手的新壓力。這兩股趨勢正迫使企業對其組織架構與策略進行重大調整。

paragraphs.en: In the tech world, many large companies are cutting their middle management teams. According to The Guardian Business, firms like Coinbase, Amazon, Block, and Meta have laid off thousands of workers. These companies believe that AI can help them do more work with fewer people. By removing layers of management, they hope to make decisions faster and save money. Anastassia Fedyk, a professor at the University of California, Berkeley, noted that this trend is becoming more common as companies find ways to move work from managers to other employees using AI tools.
paragraphs.zh: 在科技界，許多大型企業正在精簡中階管理團隊。根據《衛報商業版》（The Guardian Business）報導，像是 Coinbase、亞馬遜（Amazon）、Block 以及 Meta 等公司已裁撤數千名員工。這些企業認為，人工智慧能協助他們以更少的人力完成更多工作。透過移除管理層級，他們希望加快決策速度並節省成本。加州大學柏克萊分校（University of California, Berkeley）教授安娜斯塔西亞·費迪克（Anastassia Fedyk）指出，隨著企業找到利用人工智慧工具將工作從經理人轉移給其他員工的方法，這種趨勢正變得越來越普遍。

paragraphs.en: However, this change has created new challenges for those who remain. Prateek Singh, a former software development manager at Meta, described the situation as a type of experiment. He explained that managers now have more direct reports and are expected to do more technical work, such as writing code, alongside their management duties. According to Emily Rose McRae, an analyst at the research company Gartner, this puts a lot of pressure on managers. She warned that when managers do not have enough support, their teams also suffer, which could lead to lower quality products and services.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，這種轉變也為留任的員工帶來了新的挑戰。曾在 Meta 擔任軟體開發經理的普拉提克·辛格（Prateek Singh）將此現況形容為一種實驗。他解釋說，經理人現在需要直接管理的下屬變多了，且除了管理職責外，還被要求執行更多技術性工作，例如撰寫程式碼。根據研究公司高德納（Gartner）的分析師艾蜜莉·羅斯·麥克雷（Emily Rose McRae）表示，這對經理人造成了極大壓力。她警告，當經理人缺乏足夠的支援時，其團隊也會受到影響，這可能導致產品與服務品質下降。

paragraphs.en: Research from the data platform Revelio Labs shows that this is not just a small trend. By the end of 2025, job openings for middle managers in the United States had fallen by 42% compared to their peak in 2022. As companies continue to test these new structures, many employees are feeling the stress of a changing workplace where technology plays a much larger role than before.
paragraphs.zh: 數據平台 Revelio Labs 的研究顯示，這並非僅是小規模的趨勢。截至 2025 年底，美國中階管理職位的職缺數與 2022 年的高峰相比，已下降了 42%。隨著企業持續測試這些新架構，許多員工正感受到職場變動帶來的壓力，而科技在其中扮演的角色也比以往重要得多。

paragraphs.en: While tech companies focus on AI, the European car industry is dealing with a different kind of challenge. Sales of cars in Europe have dropped significantly since 2019, and many factories are now underused. At the same time, Chinese carmakers are rapidly growing their market share in Europe. According to automotive analyst Matthias Schmidt, Chinese car sales in western Europe nearly doubled in the first three months of the year compared to the same period in 2025.
paragraphs.zh: 當科技公司聚焦於人工智慧時，歐洲汽車產業正面臨另一種挑戰。自 2019 年以來，歐洲的汽車銷量顯著下滑，許多工廠目前處於產能過剩的狀態。與此同時，中國車廠在歐洲的市占率正迅速成長。根據汽車產業分析師馬蒂亞斯·施密特（Matthias Schmidt）指出，中國汽車在西歐的銷量，今年前三個月與 2025 年同期相比幾乎翻了一倍。

paragraphs.en: To survive, many European carmakers are now working with their Chinese rivals. Instead of closing factories and firing thousands of workers, companies like Nissan, Ford, and Stellantis are selling or sharing their production space with Chinese brands like Chery, Geely, and Leapmotor. For the European manufacturers, this cash helps solve their financial problems. However, it also shows how much the balance of power has shifted in the global car market.
paragraphs.zh: 為了生存，許多歐洲車廠現在正與中國競爭對手合作。與其關閉工廠並解僱數千名員工，日產（Nissan）、福特（Ford）與斯泰蘭蒂斯（Stellantis）等公司選擇將生產空間出售或分享給奇瑞（Chery）、吉利（Geely）與零跑汽車（Leapmotor）等中國品牌。對於歐洲製造商而言，這些資金有助於解決財務困境。然而，這也顯示出全球汽車市場的權力平衡已發生了多大的轉變。

paragraphs.en: Not every deal is easy to make. Thomas Schäfer, the chief executive of the Volkswagen brand, noted that finding the right buyer for older factories can be difficult. Meanwhile, some executives at large European car companies are privately worried. They see Chinese producers as very credible competitors who could threaten both mass-market and luxury car brands. Despite these fears, some leaders, such as Stellantis chief executive Antonio Filosa, believe that these partnerships can be helpful for both sides.
paragraphs.zh: 並非每一筆交易都容易達成。福斯（Volkswagen）品牌執行長湯瑪斯·謝弗（Thomas Schäfer）指出，要為舊工廠找到合適的買家可能很困難。與此同時，一些歐洲大型車廠的高層私下感到擔憂。他們將中國製造商視為極具威脅的競爭對手，可能危及大眾市場與豪華車品牌。儘管有這些擔憂，部分領導者，例如斯泰蘭蒂斯執行長安東尼奧·菲洛薩（Antonio Filosa），仍認為這些合作關係對雙方都有幫助。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, both the tech and auto industries are in a difficult position. Tech companies are betting that AI will lead to a more efficient future, even if it means losing many experienced managers. Meanwhile, European carmakers are trying to balance their need for survival with the reality of a global market that is becoming more competitive every day. Whether these strategies will succeed is still unclear, but it is certain that the way these industries work will never be the same again.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，科技與汽車產業皆處於艱難的境地。科技公司押注人工智慧將帶來更高效的未來，即便這意味著失去許多經驗豐富的經理人。與此同時，歐洲車廠正試圖在生存需求與日益激烈的全球市場現實之間取得平衡。這些策略是否會成功尚不明朗，但可以確定的是，這些產業的運作方式將永遠改變。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To stop employing someone, usually because there is no more work for them to do.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是常見的職場用語，指因公司營運問題而裁員，而非個人表現不佳。文中：firms like Coinbase, Amazon, Block, and Meta have laid off thousands of workers.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Employees who are directly managed by a specific person.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（報告），這裡指管理學中的「直屬部屬」。文中：He explained that managers now have more direct reports and are expected to do more technical work, such as writing code, alongside their management duties.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be certain that something is true or will happen, and to base one's actions on that belief.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 確信某事會發生，並據此採取行動（押注）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（打賭），這裡引申為對未來趨勢的判斷與投資。文中：Tech companies are betting that AI will lead to a more efficient future, even if it means losing many experienced managers.

sources.name: The Guardian Business
sources.title: ‘I didn’t want to be the guinea pig’: inside tech’s AI-fueled manager purge

sources.name: The Guardian Business
sources.title: EU carmakers pave way for Chinese rivals as balance in market shifts

quiz.question: According to the article, what specific change are middle managers in tech companies now expected to handle?

options.A: They must train AI models to replace their own positions.
options.B: They are required to perform technical tasks like coding in addition to managing people.
options.C: They are expected to move to European car companies to share their expertise.
options.D: They must oversee a smaller number of employees than they did previously.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第三段提到，前 Meta 經理 Prateek Singh 指出，經理們現在需要管理更多直接下屬，並且在管理職責之外，還必須執行編寫程式碼等技術性工作。選項 A 文章未提及；選項 C 錯誤，因為汽車業與科技業的挑戰是各自獨立的；選項 D 與文章內容相反，因為經理現在需要管理更多人，而非更少。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the current relationship between European car manufacturers and Chinese brands?

options.A: European carmakers are confident that they will remain the dominant players in the luxury market.
options.B: The partnership is seen as a long-term solution that will permanently solve financial issues for European firms.
options.C: European companies are sacrificing some control and market independence to avoid total financial collapse.
options.D: Chinese brands are exclusively interested in taking over European factories to produce their own luxury vehicles.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。從第六段與第七段可以推斷，歐洲車廠為了生存，不得不與中國競爭對手合作並分享生產空間，這顯示出權力平衡的轉移。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章提到高層對中國競爭對手感到擔憂；選項 B 過於樂觀，文章僅提到這是為了生存的策略；選項 D 錯誤，因為文章提到中國品牌也威脅到大眾市場，不僅限於豪華車。

quiz.question: In the phrase 'Chinese producers as very credible competitors,' what does the word 'credible' mean?

options.A: Capable and worthy of being taken seriously.
options.B: Likely to fail due to a lack of experience.
options.C: Honest and morally upright in their business dealings.
options.D: Small and insignificant in the global market.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此語境中，形容競爭對手為 'credible' 是指他們具有實力且構成真正的威脅，必須被認真對待。選項 B 與文章提到他們快速成長的事實矛盾；選項 C 雖然 'credible' 常指可信度，但在商業競爭語境下，這裡強調的是對手的威脅能力，而非道德感；選項 D 則完全相反，文章強調的是中國車廠市場份額的快速擴張。

quiz.question: What is the central message of this article?

options.A: AI is the only reason why global businesses are currently struggling to survive.
options.B: The European car industry is successfully outperforming Chinese rivals through new partnerships.
options.C: Large companies are finding that traditional management structures are more efficient than AI-driven models.
options.D: Major industries are undergoing significant structural transformations to adapt to new economic and technological pressures.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章開頭即點出全球經濟正經歷快速且困難的變革，隨後分別探討科技業利用 AI 重塑組織，以及汽車業透過與對手合作來應對市場份額流失，總結了這兩個產業都在進行結構性調整以應對壓力。選項 A 過於狹隘，忽略了汽車業的挑戰；選項 B 與文章內容不符；選項 C 則與文章中提到科技公司裁減管理層以追求效率的趨勢背道而馳。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
